prerequisites: A Split (Harness Feature Management & Experimentation) account with access to the data you want to extract.
An Admin API key, which is the credential the Split REST Admin API requires for most endpoints (the apiKeyType "admin" key used for the Admin API, as opposed to client-side or server-side SDK keys).
A start date (ISO-8601 timestamp) marking the earliest point from which to sync records, supplied via the start_date config field.

authMethods.name: Admin API key
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates to the Split REST Admin API using an Admin API key, provided through the api_key secret field. Split sends it in the HTTP header as `Authorization: "Bearer ADMIN_API_KEY"` (an `x-api-key` header is also accepted, but you should not include both Authorization and x-api-key on the same request). Most Split API requests are authenticated with an Admin API key; client-side and server-side SDK API keys are separate and are not used here.

setupSteps.title: Create an Admin API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Split and create an Admin API key (apiKeyType "admin", used for the Admin API) from the APIs tab in Account settings, as described on Split's Create an API Key documentation. This key authorizes requests to the REST Admin API.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the api_key config field to the Admin API key you created. The connector sends it as `Authorization: "Bearer <your key>"` on each request. Keep the key secret; its access is limited by the roles assigned to it in Split.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date config field to an ISO-8601 timestamp. The connector uses this as the earliest point from which to retrieve records.

sources.title: Split API documentation - Create an API Key
